name: pr_lex_i64min_literal
drl: rule R when P(v == -9223372036854775808) then end
_finding: i64::MIN as a constraint literal (external review round 9, finding A): the magnitude 9223372036854775808 exceeds i64::MAX, so a magnitude-first lexer rejects it before the unary minus can fold - Java folds the sign into Long.MIN_VALUE literals (JLS 3.10.1) and the oracle accepts and MATCHES the MIN-valued fact. Engine now lexes the exact magnitude as a MIN-only token folded at negation; bare (unnegated) use and magnitude+1 are compile errors on BOTH sides.
